provider_name: City of Yellowknife - Programs and Registration
notes: Main source for City recreation programs, swimming, day camps, youth activities, arts and culture. Check PerfectMind regularly.
Good source for free children programs, reading programs, story time, family events and school-break activities.
Recreational gymnastics, camps, drop-ins and competitive programs. Registration details often change by season.
Dance classes, summer programs, youth expressive arts and theatre. Check before fall/winter/summer registration periods.
Youth sailing and summer courses. Registration may also appear on Checklick.
Soccer programs for children and youth. Watch for registration windows and seasonal league updates.
Minor hockey registration, development teams, camps and seasonal updates.
Youth tennis membership, camps, clinics and open house events. Summer updates can change quickly.
Jackrabbits, youth skiing, lessons, workshops and winter programs. More active before and during ski season.
Kids programs, camps, workshops, making, technology and creative learning.
Art camps and classes for children. Email was not clearly visible from search result, so verify manually before using.
Performances, youth discounts, workshops, school and community arts programming.
Film, digital media, youth film programming and workshops. Some event details may appear first on social media.
Yoga, movement, workshops and community classes. May include kids, teens, baby-and-me or family classes depending on schedule.

provider_name: Prince of Wales Northern Heritage Centre
notes: Museum, cultural learning, family activities and public programs. Good source for free or low-cost educational activities.
Seasonal winter festival. Check heavily from January to March for kids activities, performances and family events.
Summer market source. May include youth vendors, community tables, family activities and special market events.
Youth centre and community programming. Review manually for age ranges and program suitability before publishing.
Local guiding information often appears on Facebook. Contact details should be verified before publishing specific events.
Local scouting group. Good source for outdoor, camping and youth leadership programs.
Focus: Territorial sports, KidSport, athlete funding. Activity status: Active. Facebook access: Readable. Umbrella organization with about 30 territorial sport members.
Focus: Soccer, futsal, camps, Team NWT. Activity status: Active. Facebook access: Readable. Also links Yellowknife Bay, Galaxy and Aurora Minor Soccer.
Focus: Sundogs development and youth league. Activity status: Active. Facebook access: Limited. Official website provides Summer 2026 and annual registration details.
Focus: Youth soccer development and camps. Activity status: Active. Facebook access: Limited. Website lists camps, travelling teams and one-on-one sessions.

Focus: Five-pin bowling, youth and family recreation. Activity status: Needs verification. Facebook access: Personal profile / login required. Kingpin-related Facebook source supplied by the user. The profile is not publicly readable outside Facebook login. Located at 5012 52nd Avenue, Yellowknife, with ten five-pin bowling lanes. Review visible June-September posts or screenshots before adding activities.

Focus: Community events, youth support, volunteering and fundraising. Activity status: Needs verification. Facebook access: Facebook group / login required. Facebook group supplied by the user. Posts are not publicly readable without Facebook login. Monitor for confirmed June-September family events, youth opportunities, volunteering, scholarships and fundraisers; verify dates and registration details before adding activities.

Focus: Squash, fitness, youth and family recreation. Activity status: Needs verification. Facebook access: Page found / posts not publicly readable. Yellowknife squash facility with four regulation courts. Public information indicates memberships and drop-in access are available. No verified June-September 2026 youth program dates, ages, fees or registration details were found; review Facebook posts or screenshots before adding activities.

Focus: Recreation, day camps, aquatics, library programs and community events. Activity status: Active. Facebook access: Official page / posts not publicly readable. Primary official source for municipal recreation, day camps, swimming, library programs and free community events. The June-September 2026 City events calendar was checked page by page; existing records were deduplicated and Classic Movie Monday was added.
